text.title: Content Audit Antipatterns and Agent Failure Modes
text.summary: Common audit mistakes, LLM scoring failure modes, privacy rules, and cost gotchas.

antipattern.description: Incomplete inventory from sitemap-only crawl missing orphan pages, JS-rendered SPAs, PDFs behind auth, and gated content.
antipattern.reason: Sitemap-only crawls miss 15-30% of URLs depending on site quality. Combine sitemap with log-file or GSC impressions inventory to capture orphans. Exclude gated content before LLM ingestion.

antipattern.description: Inventory keyed by URL string rather than URL hash, producing double-counting from redirects and trailing-slash variants.
antipattern.reason: Redirects and trailing slashes silently duplicate rows. Hash the canonical URL after redirect resolution and use the hash as the join key.

antipattern.description: Pages scored by LLM whose value is primarily visual (galleries, infographics, video) receive falsely low quality scores.
antipattern.reason: LLMs cannot see images. Flag image-heavy pages with a content-type classifier before scoring and route them to human review instead of automated scoring.

antipattern.description: Content less than 90 days old scored as low-traffic and flagged for removal.
antipattern.reason: GA4 data lags new content by weeks to months. Apply a recency guard: exclude any URL with a publish date less than 90 days before the audit date from traffic-based scoring.

antipattern.description: Gated or PII-containing pages sent to a cloud LLM scoring agent without exclusion.
antipattern.reason: Gated content may contain PII. Add a robots-style allowlist check upstream of the scoring agent. Never ingest content the user has not consented to share with an external model.

antipattern.description: Running Opus-tier scoring on all URLs in a large site without tiering.
antipattern.reason: 50k URLs at Opus pricing is non-trivial cost. Use Haiku for first-pass triage; escalate only borderline URLs (score 2-4) to a higher-tier model for re-scoring.
